{{Infobox ancient site | name =Maebashi Futagoyama Kofun | native_name =前橋二子山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Maebashi-futagoyama-kofun-1.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Maebashi Futagoyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Gunma Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Maebashi Futagoyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|24|41|N|139|2|5.3|E|region:JP|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Maebashi, Gunma]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =late 6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (Park) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
{{nihongo|'''Maebashi Futagoyama Kofun'''|前橋二子山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]] located in the Bunkyocho neighborhood of the city of [[Maebashi, Gunma|Maebashi]], [[Gunma Prefecture]] in the northern [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1927.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138097|title=二子山古墳|language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=August 20, 2020}}</ref> It is also sometimes referred to as the {{nihongo|'''Amagawa-Futagoyama Kofun'''|天川二子山古墳|}}.This tumuli appears in the poem "Futagoyama" by [[Sakutaro Hagiwara]].
==Overview== The Maebashi Futagoyama Kofun is located at the eastern edge of the Maebashi Plain, and is one of Asakura / Hirose cluster of ''kofun'' tumuli in the area. It has a total length of 104 meters and was built in two tiers, orientated to the northwest. Unusually, the width of the rectangular portion of the mound is larger its length. The surface was originally covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' with rows of cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]''. One anthropomorphic ''haniwa'' from this site is kept at the [[University of Tokyo]]. The remains of a 35-meter moat were discovered in an [[archaeological excavation]] in 1980 and in 1994, and an analysis of the soil covering the moat found that it contained [[volcanic ash]] from an eruption which occurred in the miqqle of the 6th century AD, indicating that the tumulus was built around the start of the sixth century. Although the detailed structure of the mound has not been investigated yet, it is believed that the [[burial chamber]] was opened in antiquity and has since collapsed. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
;Total length: 104 meters ;Anterior rectangular portion: 76 meters wide x 9.5 meters high, 2-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 72 meter diameter x 11 meters high, 2-tier
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons Category:kk]][[Category:-inline|Maebashi Futagoyama Kofun}} *[https://www.maebashi-cvb.com/spot/1041 Maebashi City official guide] {{in lang|ja}}

It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1927.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138097|title=二子山古墳|language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=August 20, 2020}}</ref> It is also sometimes referred to as the {{nihongo|'''Amagawa-Futagoyama Kofun'''|天川二子山古墳|}}.This tumuli appears in the poem "Futagoyama" by [[Sakutaro Hagiwara]].

{{Infobox ancient site | name =Maebashi Futagoyama Kofun | native_name =前橋二子山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Maebashi-futagoyama-kofun-1.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Maebashi Futagoyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Gunma Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Maebashi Futagoyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|24|41|N|139|2|5.3|E|region:JP|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Maebashi, Gunma]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =late 6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (Park) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
{{nihongo|'''Maebashi Futagoyama Kofun'''|前橋二子山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]] located in the Bunkyocho neighborhood of the city of [[Maebashi, Gunma|Maebashi]], [[Gunma Prefecture]] in the northern [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1927.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138097|title=二子山古墳|language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=August 20, 2020}}</ref> It is also sometimes referred to as the {{nihongo|'''Amagawa-Futagoyama Kofun'''|天川二子山古墳|}}.This tumuli appears in the poem "Futagoyama" by [[Sakutaro Hagiwara]].
==Overview== The Maebashi Futagoyama Kofun is located at the eastern edge of the Maebashi Plain, and is one of Asakura / Hirose cluster of ''kofun'' tumuli in the area. It has a total length of 104 meters and was built in two tiers, orientated to the northwest. Unusually, the width of the rectangular portion of the mound is larger its length. The surface was originally covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' with rows of cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]''. One anthropomorphic ''haniwa'' from this site is kept at the [[University of Tokyo]]. The remains of a 35-meter moat were discovered in an [[archaeological excavation]] in 1980 and in 1994, and an analysis of the soil covering the moat found that it contained [[volcanic ash]] from an eruption which occurred in the miqqle of the 6th century AD, indicating that the tumulus was built around the start of the sixth century. Although the detailed structure of the mound has not been investigated yet, it is believed that the [[burial chamber]] was opened in antiquity and has since collapsed. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
;Total length: 104 meters ;Anterior rectangular portion: 76 meters wide x 9.5 meters high, 2-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 72 meter diameter x 11 meters high, 2-tier

It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1927.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138097|title=二子山古墳|language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=August 20, 2020}}</ref> It is also sometimes referred to as the {{nihongo|'''Amagawa-Futagoyama Kofun'''|天川二子山古墳|}}.This tumuli appears in the poem "Futagoyama" by [[Sakutaro Hagiwara]].

{{Infobox ancient site | name =Maebashi Futagoyama Kofun | native_name =前橋二子山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Maebashi-futagoyama-kofun-1.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Maebashi Futagoyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Gunma Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Maebashi Futagoyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|24|41|N|139|2|5.3|E|region:JP|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Maebashi, Gunma]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =late 6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (Park) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
{{nihongo|'''Maebashi Futagoyama Kofun'''|前橋二子山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]] located in the Bunkyocho neighborhood of the city of [[Maebashi, Gunma|Maebashi]], [[Gunma Prefecture]] in the northern [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1927.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138097|title=二子山古墳|language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=August 20, 2020}}</ref> It is also sometimes referred to as the {{nihongo|'''Amagawa-Futagoyama Kofun'''|天川二子山古墳|}}.This tumuli appears in the poem "Futagoyama" by [[Sakutaro Hagiwara]].
==Overview== The Maebashi Futagoyama Kofun is located at the eastern edge of the Maebashi Plain, and is one of Asakura / Hirose cluster of ''kofun'' tumuli in the area. It has a total length of 104 meters and was built in two tiers, orientated to the northwest. Unusually, the width of the rectangular portion of the mound is larger its length. The surface was originally covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' with rows of cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]''. One anthropomorphic ''haniwa'' from this site is kept at the [[University of Tokyo]]. The remains of a 35-meter moat were discovered in an [[archaeological excavation]] in 1980 and in 1994, and an analysis of the soil covering the moat found that it contained [[volcanic ash]] from an eruption which occurred in the miqqle of the 6th century AD, indicating that the tumulus was built around the start of the sixth century. Although the detailed structure of the mound has not been investigated yet, it is believed that the [[burial chamber]] was opened in antiquity and has since collapsed. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
;Total length: 104 meters ;Anterior rectangular portion: 76 meters wide x 9.5 meters high, 2-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 72 meter diameter x 11 meters high, 2-tier
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)]]

It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1927.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138097|title=二子山古墳|language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=August 20, 2020}}</ref> It is also sometimes referred to as the {{nihongo|'''Amagawa-Futagoyama Kofun'''|天川二子山古墳|}}.This tumuli appears in the poem "Futagoyama" by [[Sakutaro Hagiwara]].

{{Infobox ancient site | name =Maebashi Futagoyama Kofun | native_name =前橋二子山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Maebashi-futagoyama-kofun-1.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Maebashi Futagoyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Gunma Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Maebashi Futagoyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|24|41|N|139|2|5.3|E|region:JP|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Maebashi, Gunma]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =late 6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (Park) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
{{nihongo|'''Maebashi Futagoyama Kofun'''|前橋二子山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]] located in the Bunkyocho neighborhood of the city of [[Maebashi, Gunma|Maebashi]], [[Gunma Prefecture]] in the northern [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1927.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138097|title=二子山古墳|language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=August 20, 2020}}</ref> It is also sometimes referred to as the {{nihongo|'''Amagawa-Futagoyama Kofun'''|天川二子山古墳|}}.This tumuli appears in the poem "Futagoyama" by [[Sakutaro Hagiwara]].
==Overview== The Maebashi Futagoyama Kofun is located at the eastern edge of the Maebashi Plain, and is one of Asakura / Hirose cluster of ''kofun'' tumuli in the area. It has a total length of 104 meters and was built in two tiers, orientated to the northwest. Unusually, the width of the rectangular portion of the mound is larger its length. The surface was originally covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' with rows of cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]''. One anthropomorphic ''haniwa'' from this site is kept at the [[University of Tokyo]]. The remains of a 35-meter moat were discovered in an [[archaeological excavation]] in 1980 and in 1994, and an analysis of the soil covering the moat found that it contained [[volcanic ash]] from an eruption which occurred in the miqqle of the 6th century AD, indicating that the tumulus was built around the start of the sixth century. Although the detailed structure of the mound has not been investigated yet, it is believed that the [[burial chamber]] was opened in antiquity and has since collapsed. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
;Total length: 104 meters ;Anterior rectangular portion: 76 meters wide x 9.5 meters high, 2-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 72 meter diameter x 11 meters high, 2-tier

{{nihongo|'''Maebashi Futagoyama Kofun'''|前橋二子山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Bunkyocho neighborhood of the city of [[Maebashi, Gunma|Maebashi]], [[Gunma Prefecture]] in the northern [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1927.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138097|title=二子山古墳|language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=August 20, 2020}}</ref> It is also sometimes referred to as the {{nihongo|'''Amagawa-Futagoyama Kofun'''|天川二子山古墳|}}.This tumuli appears in the poem "Futagoyama" by [[Sakutaro Hagiwara]].

It has a total length of 104 meters and was built in two tiers, orientated to the northwest. Unusually, the width of the rectangular portion of the mound is larger its length. The surface was originally covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' with rows of cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]''. One anthropomorphic ''haniwa'' from this site is kept at the [[University of Tokyo]]. The remains of a 35-meter moat were discovered in an [[archaeological excavation]] in 1980 and in 1994, and an analysis of the soil covering the moat found that it contained [[volcanic ash]] from an eruption which occurred in the miqqle of the 6th century AD, indicating that the tumulus was built around the start of the sixth century. {{Infobox ancient site | name =Maebashi Futagoyama Kofun | native_name =前橋二子山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Maebashi-futagoyama-kofun-1.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Maebashi Futagoyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Gunma Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Maebashi Futagoyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|24|41|N|139|2|5.3|E|region:JP|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Maebashi, Gunma]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =late 6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (Park) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
{{nihongo|'''Maebashi Futagoyama Kofun'''|前橋二子山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Bunkyocho neighborhood of the city of [[Maebashi, Gunma|Maebashi]], [[Gunma Prefecture]] in the northern [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1927.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138097|title=二子山古墳|language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=August 20, 2020}}</ref> It is also sometimes referred to as the {{nihongo|'''Amagawa-Futagoyama Kofun'''|天川二子山古墳|}}.This tumuli appears in the poem "Futagoyama" by [[Sakutaro Hagiwara]].
==Overview== The Maebashi Futagoyama Kofun is located at the eastern edge of the Maebashi Plain, and is one of Asakura / Hirose cluster of ''kofun'' tumuli in the area. It has a total length of 104 meters and was built in two tiers, orientated to the northwest. Unusually, the width of the rectangular portion of the mound is larger its length. The surface was originally covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' with rows of cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]''. One anthropomorphic ''haniwa'' from this site is kept at the [[University of Tokyo]]. The remains of a 35-meter moat were discovered in an [[archaeological excavation]] in 1980 and in 1994, and an analysis of the soil covering the moat found that it contained [[volcanic ash]] from an eruption which occurred in the miqqle of the 6th century AD, indicating that the tumulus was built around the start of the sixth century. Although the detailed structure of the mound has not been investigated yet, it is believed that the [[burial chamber]] was opened in antiquity and has since collapsed. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
;Total length: 104 meters ;Anterior rectangular portion: 76 meters wide x 9.5 meters high, 2-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 72 meter diameter x 11 meters high, 2-tier

{{nihongo|'''Maebashi Futagoyama Kofun'''|前橋二子山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Bunkyocho neighborhood of the city of [[Maebashi, Gunma|Maebashi]], [[Gunma Prefecture]] in the northern [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1927.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138097|title=二子山古墳|language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=August 20, 2020}}</ref> It is also sometimes referred to as the {{nihongo|'''Amagawa-Futagoyama Kofun'''|天川二子山古墳|}}.This tumuli appears in the poem "Futagoyama" by [[Sakutaro Hagiwara]].
==Overview== The Maebashi Futagoyama Kofun is located at the eastern edge of the Maebashi Plain, and is one of Asakura / Hirose cluster of ''kofun'' tumuli in the area. It has a total length of 104 meters and was built in two tiers, orientated to the northwest. Unusually, the width of the rectangular portion of the mound is larger its length. The surface was originally covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' with rows of cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]''. One anthropomorphic ''haniwa'' from this site is kept at the [[University of Tokyo]]. The remains of a 35-meter moat were discovered in an [[archaeological excavation]] in 1980 and in 1994, and an analysis of the soil covering the moat found that it contained [[volcanic ash]] from an eruption which occurred in the miqqle of the 6th century AD, indicating that the tumulus was built around the start of the sixth century. Although the detailed structure of the mound has not been investigated yet, it is believed that the [[burial chamber]] was opened in antiquity and has since collapsed. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>

{{Infobox ancient site | name =Maebashi Futagoyama Kofun | native_name =前橋二子山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Maebashi-futagoyama-kofun-1.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Maebashi Futagoyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Gunma Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Maebashi Futagoyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|24|41|N|139|2|5.3|E|region:JP|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Maebashi, Gunma]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =late 6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (Park) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
{{nihongo|'''Maebashi Futagoyama Kofun'''|前橋二子山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Bunkyocho neighborhood of the city of [[Maebashi, Gunma|Maebashi]], [[Gunma Prefecture]] in the northern [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1927.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138097|title=二子山古墳|language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=August 20, 2020}}</ref> It is also sometimes referred to as the {{nihongo|'''Amagawa-Futagoyama Kofun'''|天川二子山古墳|}}.This tumuli appears in the poem "Futagoyama" by [[Sakutaro Hagiwara]].
==Overview== The Maebashi Futagoyama Kofun is located at the eastern edge of the Maebashi Plain, and is one of Asakura / Hirose cluster of ''kofun'' tumuli in the area. It has a total length of 104 meters and was built in two tiers, orientated to the northwest. Unusually, the width of the rectangular portion of the mound is larger its length. The surface was originally covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' with rows of cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]''. One anthropomorphic ''haniwa'' from this site is kept at the [[University of Tokyo]]. The remains of a 35-meter moat were discovered in an [[archaeological excavation]] in 1980 and in 1994, and an analysis of the soil covering the moat found that it contained [[volcanic ash]] from an eruption which occurred in the miqqle of the 6th century AD, indicating that the tumulus was built around the start of the sixth century. Although the detailed structure of the mound has not been investigated yet, it is believed that the [[burial chamber]] was opened in antiquity and has since collapsed. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
;Total length: 104 meters ;Anterior rectangular portion: 76 meters wide x 9.5 meters high, 2-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 72 meter diameter x 11 meters high, 2-tier

It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1927.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138097|title=二子山古墳|language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=August 20, 2020}}</ref> It is also sometimes referred to as the {{nihongo|'''Amagawa-Futagoyama Kofun'''|天川二子山古墳|}}.This tumuli appears in the poem "Futagoyama" by [[Sakutaro Hagiwara]].

It has a total length of 104 meters and was built in two tiers, orientated to the northwest. Unusually, the width of the rectangular portion of the mound is larger its length. The surface was originally covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' with rows of cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]''. One anthropomorphic ''haniwa'' from this site is kept at the [[University of Tokyo]]. The remains of a 35-meter moat were discovered in an [[archaeological excavation]] in 1980 and in 1994, and an analysis of the soil covering the moat found that it contained [[volcanic ash]] from an eruption which occurred in the miqqle[[Category:miqqle]] of the 6th century AD, indicating that the tumulus was built around the start of the sixth century. {{Infobox ancient site | name =Maebashi Futagoyama Kofun | native_name =前橋二子山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Maebashi-futagoyama-kofun-1.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Maebashi Futagoyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Gunma Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Maebashi Futagoyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|24|41|N|139|2|5.3|E|region:JP|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Maebashi, Gunma]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =late 6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (Park) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
{{nihongo|'''Maebashi Futagoyama Kofun'''|前橋二子山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Bunkyocho neighborhood of the city of [[Maebashi, Gunma|Maebashi]], [[Gunma Prefecture]] in the northern [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1927.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138097|title=二子山古墳|language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=August 20, 2020}}</ref> It is also sometimes referred to as the {{nihongo|'''Amagawa-Futagoyama Kofun'''|天川二子山古墳|}}.This tumuli appears in the poem "Futagoyama" by [[Sakutaro Hagiwara]].
==Overview== The Maebashi Futagoyama Kofun is located at the eastern edge of the Maebashi Plain, and is one of Asakura / Hirose cluster of ''kofun'' tumuli in the area. It has a total length of 104 meters and was built in two tiers, orientated to the northwest. Unusually, the width of the rectangular portion of the mound is larger its length. The surface was originally covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' with rows of cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]''. One anthropomorphic ''haniwa'' from this site is kept at the [[University of Tokyo]]. The remains of a 35-meter moat were discovered in an [[archaeological excavation]] in 1980 and in 1994, and an analysis of the soil covering the moat found that it contained [[volcanic ash]] from an eruption which occurred in the middle[[Category:middle]] of the 6th century AD, indicating that the tumulus was built around the start of the sixth century. Although the detailed structure of the mound has not been investigated yet, it is believed that the [[burial chamber]] was opened in antiquity and has since collapsed. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
;Total length: 104 meters ;Anterior rectangular portion: 76 meters wide x 9.5 meters high, 2-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 72 meter diameter x 11 meters high, 2-tier
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)]]

It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1927.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138097|title=二子山古墳|language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=August 20, 2020}}</ref> It is also sometimes referred to as the {{nihongo|'''Amagawa-Futagoyama Kofun'''|天川二子山古墳|}}.This tumuli appears in the poem "Futagoyama" by [[Sakutaro Hagiwara]].

{{Infobox ancient site | name =Maebashi Futagoyama Kofun | native_name =前橋二子山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Maebashi-futagoyama-kofun-1.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Maebashi Futagoyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Gunma Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Maebashi Futagoyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|24|41|N|139|2|5.3|E|region:JP|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Maebashi, Gunma]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =late 6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (Park) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
{{nihongo|'''Maebashi Futagoyama Kofun'''|前橋二子山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Bunkyocho neighborhood of the city of [[Maebashi, Gunma|Maebashi]], [[Gunma Prefecture]] in the northern [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1927.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138097|title=二子山古墳|language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=August 20, 2020}}</ref> It is also sometimes referred to as the {{nihongo|'''Amagawa-Futagoyama Kofun'''|天川二子山古墳|}}.This tumuli appears in the poem "Futagoyama" by [[Sakutaro Hagiwara]].
==Overview== The Maebashi Futagoyama Kofun is located at the eastern edge of the Maebashi Plain, and is one of Asakura / Hirose cluster of ''kofun'' tumuli in the area. It has a total length of 104 meters and was built in two tiers, orientated to the northwest. Unusually, the width of the rectangular portion of the mound is larger its length. The surface was originally covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' with rows of cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]''. One anthropomorphic ''haniwa'' from this site is kept at the [[University of Tokyo]]. The remains of a 35-meter moat were discovered in an [[archaeological excavation]] in 1980 and in 1994, and an analysis of the soil covering the moat found that it contained [[volcanic ash]] from an eruption which occurred in the middle[[Category:middle]] of the 6th century AD, indicating that the tumulus was built around the start of the sixth century. Although the detailed structure of the mound has not been investigated yet, it is believed that the [[burial chamber]] was opened in antiquity and has since collapsed. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
;Total length: 104 meters ;Anterior rectangular portion: 76 meters wide x 9.5 meters high, 2-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 72 meter diameter x 11 meters high, 2-tier
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)]]

{{Infobox ancient site | name =Maebashi Futagoyama Kofun | native_name =前橋二子山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Maebashi-futagoyama-kofun-1.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Maebashi Futagoyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Gunma Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Maebashi Futagoyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|24|41|N|139|2|5.3|E|region:JP|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Maebashi, Gunma]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =late 6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (Park) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
{{nihongo|'''Maebashi Futagoyama Kofun'''|前橋二子山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Bunkyocho neighborhood of the city of [[Maebashi, Gunma|Maebashi]], [[Gunma Prefecture]] in the northern [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1927.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138097|title=二子山古墳|language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=August 20, 2020}}</ref> It is also sometimes referred to as the {{nihongo|'''Amagawa-Futagoyama Kofun'''|天川二子山古墳|}}.This tumuli appears in the poem "Futagoyama" by [[Sakutaro Hagiwara]].
==Overview== The Maebashi Futagoyama Kofun is located at the eastern edge of the Maebashi Plain, and is one of Asakura / Hirose cluster of ''kofun'' tumuli in the area. It has a total length of 104 meters and was built in two tiers, orientated to the northwest. Unusually, the width of the rectangular portion of the mound is larger its length. The surface was originally covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' with rows of cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]''. One anthropomorphic ''haniwa'' from this site is kept at the [[University of Tokyo]]. The remains of a 35-meter moat were discovered in an [[archaeological excavation]] in 1980 and in 1994, and an analysis of the soil covering the moat found that it contained [[volcanic ash]] from an eruption which occurred in the middle[[Category:middle]] of the 6th century AD, indicating that the tumulus was built around the start of the sixth century. Although the detailed structure of the mound has not been investigated yet, it is believed that the [[burial chamber]] was opened in antiquity and has since collapsed. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
;Total length: 104 meters ;Anterior rectangular portion: 76 meters wide x 9.5 meters high, 2-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 72 meter diameter x 11 meters high, 2-tier
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)]]

{{Infobox ancient site | name =Maebashi Futagoyama Kofun | native_name =前橋二子山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Maebashi-futagoyama-kofun-1.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Maebashi Futagoyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Gunma Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Maebashi Futagoyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|24|41|N|139|2|5.3|E|region:JP|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Maebashi, Gunma]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =late 6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (Park) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
{{nihongo|'''Maebashi Futagoyama Kofun'''|前橋二子山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Bunkyocho neighborhood of the city of [[Maebashi, Gunma|Maebashi]], [[Gunma Prefecture]] in the northern [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1927.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138097|title=二子山古墳|language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=August 20, 2020}}</ref> It is also sometimes referred to as the {{nihongo|'''Amagawa-Futagoyama Kofun'''|天川二子山古墳|}}.This tumuli appears in the poem "Futagoyama" by [[Sakutaro Hagiwara]].
==Overview== The Maebashi Futagoyama Kofun is located at the eastern edge of the Maebashi Plain, and is one of Asakura / Hirose cluster of ''kofun'' tumuli in the area. It has a total length of 104 meters and was built in two tiers, orientated to the northwest. Unusually, the width of the rectangular portion of the mound is larger its length. The surface was originally covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' with rows of cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]''. One anthropomorphic ''haniwa'' from this site is kept at the [[University of Tokyo]]. The remains of a 35-meter moat were discovered in an [[archaeological excavation]] in 1980 and in 1994, and an analysis of the soil covering the moat found that it contained [[volcanic ash]] from an eruption which occurred in the middle[[Category:middle]] of the 6th century AD, indicating that the tumulus was built around the start of the sixth century. Although the detailed structure of the mound has not been investigated yet, it is believed that the [[burial chamber]] was opened in antiquity and has since collapsed. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
;Total length: 104 meters ;Anterior rectangular portion: 76 meters wide x 9.5 meters high, 2-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 72 meter diameter x 11 meters high, 2-tier
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons Category:kk]][[Category:-inline|Maebashi Futagoyama Kofun}} *[https://www.maebashi-cvb.com/spot/1041 Maebashi City official guide] {{in lang|ja}}

{{Infobox ancient site | name =Maebashi Futagoyama Kofun | native_name =前橋二子山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Maebashi-futagoyama-kofun-1.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Maebashi Futagoyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Gunma Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Maebashi Futagoyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|24|41|N|139|2|5.3|E|region:JP|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Maebashi, Gunma]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = [[kofun]] | Group = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =late 6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (Park) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
{{nihongo|'''Maebashi Futagoyama Kofun'''|前橋二子山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Bunkyocho neighborhood of the city of [[Maebashi, Gunma|Maebashi]], [[Gunma Prefecture]] in the northern [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1927.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138097|title=二子山古墳|language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=August 20, 2020}}</ref> It is also sometimes referred to as the {{nihongo|'''Amagawa-Futagoyama Kofun'''|天川二子山古墳|}}.This tumuli appears in the poem "Futagoyama" by [[Sakutaro Hagiwara]].
==Overview== The Maebashi Futagoyama Kofun is located at the eastern edge of the Maebashi Plain, and is one of Asakura / Hirose cluster of ''kofun'' tumuli in the area. It has a total length of 104 meters and was built in two tiers, orientated to the northwest. Unusually, the width of the rectangular portion of the mound is larger its length. The surface was originally covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' with rows of cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]''. One anthropomorphic ''haniwa'' from this site is kept at the [[University of Tokyo]]. The remains of a 35-meter moat were discovered in an [[archaeological excavation]] in 1980 and in 1994, and an analysis of the soil covering the moat found that it contained [[volcanic ash]] from an eruption which occurred in the middleof the 6th century AD, indicating that the tumulus was built around the start of the sixth century. Although the detailed structure of the mound has not been investigated yet, it is believed that the [[burial chamber]] was opened in antiquity and has since collapsed. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
;Total length: 104 meters ;Anterior rectangular portion: 76 meters wide x 9.5 meters high, 2-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 72 meter diameter x 11 meters high, 2-tier
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)]]
==References== {{reflist}}

It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1927.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138097|title=二子山古墳|language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=August 20, 2020}}</ref> It is also sometimes referred to as the {{nihongo|'''Amagawa-Futagoyama Kofun'''|天川二子山古墳|}}.This tumuli appears in the poem "Futagoyama" by [[Sakutaro Hagiwara]].

Although the detailed structure of the mound has not been investigated yet, it is believed that the [[burial chamber]] was opened in antiquity and has since collapsed. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>

{{Infobox ancient site | name =Maebashi Futagoyama Kofun | native_name =前橋二子山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Maebashi-futagoyama-kofun-1.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Maebashi Futagoyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Gunma Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Maebashi Futagoyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|24|41|N|139|2|5.3|E|region:JP|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Maebashi, Gunma]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =late 6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (Park) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
{{nihongo|'''Maebashi Futagoyama Kofun'''|前橋二子山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Bunkyocho neighborhood of the city of [[Maebashi, Gunma|Maebashi]], [[Gunma Prefecture]] in the northern [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1927.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138097|title=二子山古墳|language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=August 20, 2020}}</ref> It is also sometimes referred to as the {{nihongo|'''Amagawa-Futagoyama Kofun'''|天川二子山古墳|}}.This tumuli appears in the poem "Futagoyama" by [[Sakutaro Hagiwara]].
==Overview== The Maebashi Futagoyama Kofun is located at the eastern edge of the Maebashi Plain, and is one of Asakura / Hirose cluster of ''kofun'' tumuli in the area. It has a total length of 104 meters and was built in two tiers, orientated to the northwest. Unusually, the width of the rectangular portion of the mound is larger its length. The surface was originally covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' with rows of cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]''. One anthropomorphic ''haniwa'' from this site is kept at the [[University of Tokyo]]. The remains of a 35-meter moat were discovered in an [[archaeological excavation]] in 1980 and in 1994, and an analysis of the soil covering the moat found that it contained [[volcanic ash]] from an eruption which occurred in the middleof the 6th century AD, indicating that the tumulus was built around the start of the sixth century. Although the detailed structure of the mound has not been investigated yet, it is believed that the [[burial chamber]] was opened in antiquity and has since collapsed. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
;Total length: 104 meters ;Anterior rectangular portion: 76 meters wide x 9.5 meters high, 2-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 72 meter diameter x 11 meters high, 2-tier
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)]]

{{nihongo|'''Maebashi Futagoyama Kofun'''|前橋二子山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Bunkyocho neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Maebashi, Gunma|Maebashi]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]] in the northern [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1927.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138097|title=二子山古墳|language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=August 20, 2020}}</ref> It is also sometimes referred to as the {{nihongo|'''Amagawa-Futagoyama Kofun'''|天川二子山古墳|}}.This tumuli appears in the poem "Futagoyama" by [[:en:Sakutaro Hagiwara|Sakutaro Hagiwara]].
==Overview== The Maebashi Futagoyama Kofun is located at the eastern edge of the Maebashi Plain, and is one of Asakura / Hirose cluster of ''kofun'' tumuli in the area. It has a total length of 104 meters and was built in two tiers, orientated to the northwest. Unusually, the width of the rectangular portion of the mound is larger its length. The surface was originally covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' with rows of cylindrical ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]''. One anthropomorphic ''haniwa'' from this site is kept at the [[:en:University of Tokyo|University of Tokyo]]. The remains of a 35-meter moat were discovered in an [[archaeological excavation]] in 1980 and in 1994, and an analysis of the soil covering the moat found that it contained [[:en:Volcanic ash|Volcanic ash]] from an eruption which occurred in the middleof the 6th century AD, indicating that the tumulus was built around the start of the sixth century. Although the detailed structure of the mound has not been investigated yet, it is believed that the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was opened in antiquity and has since collapsed. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Maebashi Futagoyama Kofun]][[en:Maebashi Futagoyama Kofun]][[ja:前橋二子山古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17221378]] Wikidata name: Maebashi-futagoyama-kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:56, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name =Maebashi Futagoyama Kofun | native_name =前橋二子山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Maebashi-futagoyama-kofun-1.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Maebashi Futagoyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Gunma Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Maebashi Futagoyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|36.3765|N|139.08863888889|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Maebashi|qid=Q201613}} | region = [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =late 6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (Park) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
{{nihongo|'''Maebashi Futagoyama Kofun'''|前橋二子山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Bunkyocho neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Maebashi, Gunma|Maebashi]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]] in the northern [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1927.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138097|title=二子山古墳|language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=August 20, 2020}}</ref> It is also sometimes referred to as the {{nihongo|'''Amagawa-Futagoyama Kofun'''|天川二子山古墳|}}.This tumuli appears in the poem "Futagoyama" by [[:en:Sakutaro Hagiwara|Sakutaro Hagiwara]].
==Overview== The Maebashi Futagoyama Kofun is located at the eastern edge of the Maebashi Plain, and is one of Asakura / Hirose cluster of ''kofun'' tumuli in the area. It has a total length of 104 meters and was built in two tiers, orientated to the northwest. Unusually, the width of the rectangular portion of the mound is larger its length. The surface was originally covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' with rows of cylindrical ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]''. One anthropomorphic ''haniwa'' from this site is kept at the [[:en:University of Tokyo|University of Tokyo]]. The remains of a 35-meter moat were discovered in an [[archaeological excavation]] in 1980 and in 1994, and an analysis of the soil covering the moat found that it contained [[:en:Volcanic ash|Volcanic ash]] from an eruption which occurred in the middleof the 6th century AD, indicating that the tumulus was built around the start of the sixth century. Although the detailed structure of the mound has not been investigated yet, it is believed that the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was opened in antiquity and has since collapsed. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
;Total length: 104 meters ;Anterior rectangular portion: 76 meters wide x 9.5 meters high, 2-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 72 meter diameter x 11 meters high, 2-tier
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)]]
==References== {{reflist}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Maebashi Futagoyama Kofun]][[en:Maebashi Futagoyama Kofun]][[ja:前橋二子山古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17221378]] Wikidata name: Maebashi-futagoyama-kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:56, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name =Maebashi Futagoyama Kofun | native_name =前橋二子山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Maebashi-futagoyama-kofun-1.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Maebashi Futagoyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Gunma Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Maebashi Futagoyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|36.3765|N|139.08863888889|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Maebashi|WD=Q201613}} | region = [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =late 6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (Park) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
{{nihongo|'''Maebashi Futagoyama Kofun'''|前橋二子山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Bunkyocho neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Maebashi, Gunma|Maebashi]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]] in the northern [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1927.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138097|title=二子山古墳|language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=August 20, 2020}}</ref> It is also sometimes referred to as the {{nihongo|'''Amagawa-Futagoyama Kofun'''|天川二子山古墳|}}.This tumuli appears in the poem "Futagoyama" by [[:en:Sakutaro Hagiwara|Sakutaro Hagiwara]].
==Overview== The Maebashi Futagoyama Kofun is located at the eastern edge of the Maebashi Plain, and is one of Asakura / Hirose cluster of ''kofun'' tumuli in the area. It has a total length of 104 meters and was built in two tiers, orientated to the northwest. Unusually, the width of the rectangular portion of the mound is larger its length. The surface was originally covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' with rows of cylindrical ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]''. One anthropomorphic ''haniwa'' from this site is kept at the [[:en:University of Tokyo|University of Tokyo]]. The remains of a 35-meter moat were discovered in an [[archaeological excavation]] in 1980 and in 1994, and an analysis of the soil covering the moat found that it contained [[:en:Volcanic ash|Volcanic ash]] from an eruption which occurred in the middleof the 6th century AD, indicating that the tumulus was built around the start of the sixth century. Although the detailed structure of the mound has not been investigated yet, it is believed that the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was opened in antiquity and has since collapsed. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
;Total length: 104 meters ;Anterior rectangular portion: 76 meters wide x 9.5 meters high, 2-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 72 meter diameter x 11 meters high, 2-tier
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons category-inline|Maebashi Futagoyama Kofun}} *[https://www.maebashi-cvb.com/spot/1041 Maebashi City official guide] {{in lang|ja}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Maebashi Futagoyama Kofun]][[en:Maebashi Futagoyama Kofun]][[ja:前橋二子山古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Maebashi Futagoyama Kofun]][[en:Maebashi Futagoyama Kofun]][[ja:前橋二子山古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17221378]] Wikidata name: Maebashi-futagoyama-kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:56, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name =Maebashi Futagoyama Kofun | native_name =前橋二子山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Maebashi-futagoyama-kofun-1.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Maebashi Futagoyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Gunma Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Maebashi Futagoyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|36.3765|N|139.08863888889|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Maebashi|WD=Q201613}} | region = [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =late 6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (Park) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
{{nihongo|'''Maebashi Futagoyama Kofun'''|前橋二子山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Bunkyocho neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Maebashi, Gunma|Maebashi]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]] in the northern [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1927.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138097|title=二子山古墳|language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=August 20, 2020}}</ref> It is also sometimes referred to as the {{nihongo|'''Amagawa-Futagoyama Kofun'''|天川二子山古墳|}}.This tumuli appears in the poem "Futagoyama" by [[:en:Sakutaro Hagiwara|Sakutaro Hagiwara]].
==Overview== The Maebashi Futagoyama Kofun is located at the eastern edge of the Maebashi Plain, and is one of Asakura / Hirose cluster of ''kofun'' tumuli in the area. It has a total length of 104 meters and was built in two tiers, orientated to the northwest. Unusually, the width of the rectangular portion of the mound is larger its length. The surface was originally covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' with rows of cylindrical ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]''. One anthropomorphic ''haniwa'' from this site is kept at the [[:en:University of Tokyo|University of Tokyo]]. The remains of a 35-meter moat were discovered in an [[archaeological excavation]] in 1980 and in 1994, and an analysis of the soil covering the moat found that it contained [[:en:Volcanic ash|Volcanic ash]] from an eruption which occurred in the middleof the 6th century AD, indicating that the tumulus was built around the start of the sixth century. Although the detailed structure of the mound has not been investigated yet, it is believed that the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was opened in antiquity and has since collapsed. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
;Total length: 104 meters ;Anterior rectangular portion: 76 meters wide x 9.5 meters high, 2-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 72 meter diameter x 11 meters high, 2-tier
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Maebashi Futagoyama Kofun]][[en:Maebashi Futagoyama Kofun]][[ja:前橋二子山古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Maebashi Futagoyama Kofun]][[en:Maebashi Futagoyama Kofun]][[ja:前橋二子山古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Maebashi Futagoyama Kofun]][[en:Maebashi Futagoyama Kofun]][[ja:前橋二子山古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17221378]] Wikidata name: Maebashi-futagoyama-kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:56, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name =Maebashi Futagoyama Kofun | native_name =前橋二子山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Maebashi-futagoyama-kofun-1.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Maebashi Futagoyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Gunma Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Maebashi Futagoyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|36.3765|N|139.08863888889|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Maebashi|WD=Q201613}} | region = [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =late 6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (Park) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
{{nihongo|'''Maebashi Futagoyama Kofun'''|前橋二子山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Bunkyocho neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Maebashi, Gunma|Maebashi]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]] in the northern [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1927.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138097|title=二子山古墳|language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=August 20, 2020}}</ref> It is also sometimes referred to as the {{nihongo|'''Amagawa-Futagoyama Kofun'''|天川二子山古墳|}}.This tumuli appears in the poem "Futagoyama" by [[:en:Sakutaro Hagiwara|Sakutaro Hagiwara]].
==Overview== The Maebashi Futagoyama Kofun is located at the eastern edge of the Maebashi Plain, and is one of Asakura / Hirose cluster of ''kofun'' tumuli in the area. It has a total length of 104 meters and was built in two tiers, orientated to the northwest. Unusually, the width of the rectangular portion of the mound is larger its length. The surface was originally covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' with rows of cylindrical ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]''. One anthropomorphic ''haniwa'' from this site is kept at the [[:en:University of Tokyo|University of Tokyo]]. The remains of a 35-meter moat were discovered in an [[archaeological excavation]] in 1980 and in 1994, and an analysis of the soil covering the moat found that it contained [[:en:Volcanic ash|Volcanic ash]] from an eruption which occurred in the middleof the 6th century AD, indicating that the tumulus was built around the start of the sixth century. Although the detailed structure of the mound has not been investigated yet, it is believed that the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was opened in antiquity and has since collapsed. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
;Total length: 104 meters ;Anterior rectangular portion: 76 meters wide x 9.5 meters high, 2-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 72 meter diameter x 11 meters high, 2-tier
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)]]

Although the detailed structure of the mound has not been investigated yet, it is believed that the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was opened in antiquity and has since collapsed. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>

It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1927.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138097|title=二子山古墳|language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=August 20, 2020}}</ref> It is also sometimes referred to as the {{nihongo|'''Amagawa-Futagoyama Kofun'''|天川二子山古墳|}}.This tumuli appears in the poem "Futagoyama" by [[:en:Sakutaro Hagiwara|Sakutaro Hagiwara]].

It has a total length of 104 meters and was built in two tiers, orientated to the northwest. Unusually, the width of the rectangular portion of the mound is larger its length. The surface was originally covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' with rows of cylindrical ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}''. One anthropomorphic ''haniwa'' from this site is kept at the {{ill|University of Tokyo|en|University of Tokyo|WD=Q7842|lt=University of Tokyo}}. The remains of a 35-meter moat were discovered in an [[archaeological excavation]] in 1980 and in 1994, and an analysis of the soil covering the moat found that it contained {{ill|Volcanic ash|en|Volcanic ash|WD=Q107099|lt=Volcanic ash}} from an eruption which occurred in the middleof the 6th century AD, indicating that the tumulus was built around the start of the sixth century. Although the detailed structure of the mound has not been investigated yet, it is believed that the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was opened in antiquity and has since collapsed. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>

{{Infobox ancient site | name =Maebashi Futagoyama Kofun | native_name =前橋二子山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Maebashi-futagoyama-kofun-1.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Maebashi Futagoyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Gunma Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Maebashi Futagoyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|36.3765|N|139.08863888889|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Maebashi|WD=Q201613}} | region = {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|WD=Q132480|lt=Kantō region}} | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =late 6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (Park) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
{{nihongo|'''Maebashi Futagoyama Kofun'''|前橋二子山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Bunkyocho neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Maebashi, Gunma|Maebashi]], {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|en|Gunma Prefecture|WD=Q129499|lt=Gunma Prefecture}} in the northern {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|WD=Q132480|lt=Kantō region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1927.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138097|title=二子山古墳|language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=August 20, 2020}}</ref> It is also sometimes referred to as the {{nihongo|'''Amagawa-Futagoyama Kofun'''|天川二子山古墳|}}.This tumuli appears in the poem "Futagoyama" by [[:en:Sakutaro Hagiwara|Sakutaro Hagiwara]].
==Overview== The Maebashi Futagoyama Kofun is located at the eastern edge of the Maebashi Plain, and is one of Asakura / Hirose cluster of ''kofun'' tumuli in the area. It has a total length of 104 meters and was built in two tiers, orientated to the northwest. Unusually, the width of the rectangular portion of the mound is larger its length. The surface was originally covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' with rows of cylindrical ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}''. One anthropomorphic ''haniwa'' from this site is kept at the {{ill|University of Tokyo|en|University of Tokyo|WD=Q7842|lt=University of Tokyo}}. The remains of a 35-meter moat were discovered in an [[archaeological excavation]] in 1980 and in 1994, and an analysis of the soil covering the moat found that it contained {{ill|Volcanic ash|en|Volcanic ash|WD=Q107099|lt=Volcanic ash}} from an eruption which occurred in the middleof the 6th century AD, indicating that the tumulus was built around the start of the sixth century. Although the detailed structure of the mound has not been investigated yet, it is believed that the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was opened in antiquity and has since collapsed. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
;Total length: 104 meters ;Anterior rectangular portion: 76 meters wide x 9.5 meters high, 2-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 72 meter diameter x 11 meters high, 2-tier
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)|WD=Q16248234|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)}}
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons category-inline|Maebashi Futagoyama Kofun}} *[https://www.maebashi-cvb.com/spot/1041 Maebashi City official guide] {{in lang|ja}}

Although the detailed structure of the mound has not been investigated yet, it is believed that the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was opened in antiquity and has since collapsed. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>

It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1927.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138097|title=二子山古墳|language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=August 20, 2020}}</ref> It is also sometimes referred to as the {{nihongo|'''Amagawa-Futagoyama Kofun'''|天川二子山古墳|}}.This tumuli appears in the poem "Futagoyama" by [[:en:Sakutaro Hagiwara|Sakutaro Hagiwara]].
